task_completion.description: Search for San Francisco International Airport
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar in the Sygic GPS app contains 'San Francisco International Airport' or 'SFO', or the map displays search results for the airport.

task_completion.description: Open location details for the airport instead of navigating directly
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the location details panel for SFO (which may include buttons like 'Parking', 'Nearby', or 'Explore') rather than an active navigation route to the generic airport drop-off.

task_completion.description: Search for or display parking facilities near the airport
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen displays a list of search results or map pins specifically for parking facilities (e.g., containing keywords 'Parking', 'Garage', or 'Lot') in the vicinity of SFO.

task_completion.description: Select a specific parking garage or lot as the destination
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific parking facility at SFO (such as 'International Garage', 'Long-Term Parking Garage', or 'Long-Term Surface Lot') is selected as the target destination on the map or details panel.

task_completion.description: Initiate navigation to the specific parking facility
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app is in active navigation mode (showing a highlighted route line, ETA, and turn-by-turn instructions) and the destination is clearly the chosen parking facility, not the generic airport.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Sygic GPS navigation app
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sygic app interface (com.sygic.aura) with TomTom map data, rather than a web browser, Google Maps, or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GUI rather than using terminal commands to spoof success
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a logical progression of GUI screens (search -> details -> parking -> route) rather than a sudden jump to a final state via terminal intents.

integrity.description: The navigation route appears genuinely calculated from the starting location
integrity.visual_evidence: The route originates from the mock location (Mountain View / Googleplex area, approx 39 miles away) and shows realistic ETA and distance metrics.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using the Sygic GPS app. The starting location is mocked to Mountain View, CA (Googleplex). The agent MUST NOT navigate to the generic 'San Francisco International Airport' destination. Instead, they must open the airport's location details and use the Parking/Nearby features to find and navigate to a specific parking facility. Valid destinations will include keywords like 'Parking', 'Garage', or 'Lot' (e.g., 'International Garage', 'Long-Term Parking Garage', 'Long-Term Surface Lot').
